_comment: WC2026 Group A, MD3 (simultaneous). Standings before: Mexico 6 pts (2-0 South Africa, 1-0 South Korea) ALREADY THROUGH and likely group winner; South Korea 3, Czech Republic 1 (1-2 South Korea, 1-1 South Africa), South Africa 1. Mexico qualified -> ROTATION / lower urgency likely; Czech must win (and need favours) to chase 3rd. This compresses the gap: Mexico still favourite but with a qualified-team discount. Cards not modelled (referee TBD).

meta.stakes: Mexico already qualified (6 pts) and may rotate; Czech Republic (1 pt) must win to keep slim third-place hopes alive.

home.name: Mexico
notes: Strong host, won both (3-0 aggregate, clean sheets). attack 1.22.
Already through -> likely rotation/managed minutes, context 0.92.

away.name: Czech Republic
notes: Decent European side; lost narrowly to South Korea, drew South Africa. attack 1.0.

_postmatch.result: Mexico 3-0 Czech Republic. Already-qualified Mexico routed; clean sheet.
_postmatch.verdict: 1X2 hit (Mexico won) but for the WRONG reasons on texture: I applied a rotation/lower-urgency DISCOUNT (context 0.92, win prob compressed to 45.5%) because Mexico were already through -- and they came out and won 3-0. The discount was unjustified; Mexico overperformed (1.45 -> 3) and kept a clean sheet. Central read (tight 1-1/1-0) was the opposite of a 3-0 rout.
_postmatch.lesson: The qualified-team rotation discount was wrong, the same day the eliminated-team motivation downgrade (South Korea-South Africa) was also wrong. Motivation/qualification adjustments are speculative and backfired both ways today -- a qualified host can still want to win big, an eliminated side can still spoil. Trust the strength/market baseline; reserve motivation tweaks for confirmed rotation news, not scenario guesses.
